items.title: S&P 500 dips slightly amid tech valuation concerns, JPMorgan raises year-end target to 7,800
items.content: S&P 500 closed at 7,358.22 on June 24, down 0.1%. Earlier in June S&P 500 crossed 7,500 for first time. Tech stocks pulling back on AI spending sustainability concerns. JPMorgan raised year-end target to 7,800.

items.title: Hang Seng Index rebounds after 5-day losing streak, closes at 23,412
items.content: Hang Seng Index closed at 23,412.18 on June 24, up 0.33%, breaking a five-day losing streak. Past month index declined 8.54%. Old economy stocks outperformed Chinese internet stocks.

items.title: HK$255 billion IPO lock-up expiry looms over Hong Kong market in July
items.content: A significant HK$255 billion in IPO and secondary share lock-ups set to expire in July 2026, potentially adding market pressure and volatility to Hong Kong equities.

items.title: April core PCE at 3.3%, May data due June 25 - Fed forecast 3.3% for 2026
items.content: April core PCE stands at 3.3% YoY, well above Fed's 2% target. May PCE data scheduled for release June 25. Fed's June SEP forecasts core PCE at 3.3% for full year 2026.

items.title: Iran announces Strait of Hormuz closure, US denies - record 17M barrels transit despite tensions
items.content: Iran claimed closure of Strait of Hormuz on June 20 citing Israeli attacks on Lebanon. US Central Command confirmed 55 merchant ships and 17 million barrels of oil transited on June 21, a single-day record. 60-day ceasefire agreement signed June 19 in Geneva. Iran and Oman discussing joint strait administration.

items.title: China to invest $295 billion in AI data centers over next 5 years under 15th Five-Year Plan
items.content: China planning approximately $295 billion allocation over five years for AI data center buildout. Biren Technology surged after $717M Hong Kong IPO. CSRC signaling crackdown on illegal AI-related trading speculation.

items.title: Earnings this week: Micron, FedEx, Carnival, Paychex, BlackBerry, Trip.com
items.content: Micron expected EPS $20.05 on 276% revenue surge from memory chip supply-demand imbalance. FedEx reported FQ4 revenue $25B (+13% YoY), adj EPS $6.31 beat estimates. Carnival, Paychex, BlackBerry, Trip.com, KB Home also reporting.

items.title: Global semiconductor market projected to surge nearly 90% to $1.511 trillion in 2026
items.content: AI demand driving semiconductor market to $1.511T projected size. However over $1.3T wiped from global chip sector earlier in June after Broadcom revenue miss, highlighting sensitivity of AI chip valuations.

items.title: Hong Kong market split: old economy stocks surge while Chinese internet stocks fall
items.content: Techtronic Industries, Orient Overseas, Sun Hung Kai Properties, Henderson Land among top performers. Kuaishou, Meituan, Alibaba, Tencent saw significant declines.

items.title: Biren Technology hits new highs after $717M Hong Kong IPO; China's domestic AI chip ecosystem improving
items.content: Biren Technology (GPU-like AI chips) surged post-IPO. SMIC capable of 7nm but constrained by US restrictions. Cambricon reported first annual profit in 2025. China still years away from globally competitive independent AI chips.

items.title: VP Vance: Strait of Hormuz remains open; US and Iran sign 60-day ceasefire deal in Geneva
items.content: Preliminary US-Iran agreement to lift all restrictions on movement through Strait of Hormuz. 60-day ceasefire signed June 19 in Geneva. JD Vance confirmed strait open. Record oil transit volume recorded.

items.title: JPMorgan raises S&P 500 year-end target to 7,800, cites AI-driven earnings growth
items.content: Despite June tech pullback, JPMorgan bullish on year-end S&P 500 at 7,800, citing AI-driven earnings growth and resilient economy.
